description_long: This is a matte black stovetop espresso maker, commonly known as a Moka pot, featuring a faceted, polygonal body with a distinct octagonal shape. The upper chamber is slightly smaller and sits atop a wider lower chamber, both joined by a central band with a visible safety valve. A prominent, angular side-mounted handle made of black plastic is attached to the upper chamber, opposite a short, sharply tapered spout for pouring. The lid is flat with a small, faceted knob for easy lifting, and appears to be removable. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, with no visible decorative patterns, emphasizing function over ornamentation. The surface finish is matte, giving the object a subdued, non-reflective appearance.
description: A matte black, octagonal stovetop espresso maker with a side-mounted handle, short spout, and flat lid with a knob. The design is plain and functional, with a faceted body and no decorative patterns.
description_view_0: The front view shows the spout, handle, and lid knob clearly, highlighting the faceted body and the safety valve on the lower chamber.
description_view_1: The rear view centers the handle, showing its angular attachment and the back of the lid, with the spout not visible.
description_view_2: The side view displays the spout in profile and the handle extending backward, emphasizing the geometric shape of the body.
description_view_3: The opposite side view shows the back of the spout and the safety valve, with the handle mostly hidden.
